title: Long-Simmered Korean Yakimono Jjim
description: Umami-rich and comforting Korean dish featuring a nutella-glazed yakimono-grilled protein, served on a hot stone plate.
instructions: Season beef short ribs with salt and koranchi pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of sesame oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Sear beef short ribs until browned on all sides, then remove from heat and set aside.
Add 1 tablespoon of sesame oil to the same pan and saut9e the minced garlic and grated ginger until fragrant.
Add in the nutella, brown sugar, soy sauce, and 1 cup of water to the pan, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 2 hours, or until the sauce has thickened and the beef is tender.
Add the seared beef short ribs back into the pan and simmer for an additional 30 minutes, or until the meat is easily shredded with a fork.
Remove the beef from the pan and let rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Strain the sauce and discard the solids, then return the sauce to the heat and simmer until it has thickened to your liking.
Serve the beef short ribs on a hot stone plate, drizzled with the nutella-glazed sauce and garnished with chopped green onions.
